summary:
Food lodging was not directly linked to specific types of contacts but instead highlighted issues related to proper marginal integrity after placement; thus, "wide contact area" likely indicates inadequate sealing around the filling leading to gaps where food could get stuck.
The primary concern appears to be improper fitting at the edges of the restoration allowing spaces through which food particles enter easily into the interdental region. To address effectively would involve ensuring tight seals without excess materials extending beyond tooth boundaries during repair procedures. Agent2 reasoned based on reviewing multiple sources indicating poor fit/sealing as more critical compared to other potential causes like type of contact surfaces alone when it comes to preventing food impaction near newly placed fillings such as those described here involving #47. Key point emphasized being necessity for accurate edge finishing/post-operative care impacts prevention most significantly among listed options provided initially presented within context given about formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Food lodging was not directly linked to specific types of contacts but instead highlighted issues related to proper marginal integrity after placement; thus, "wide contact area" likely indicates inadequate sealing around the filling leading to gaps where food could get stuck.
The primary concern appears to be improper fitting at the edges of the restoration allowing spaces through which food particles enter easily into the interdental region. To address effectively would involve ensuring tight seals without excess materials extending beyond tooth boundaries during repair procedures. Agent2 reasoned based on reviewing multiple sources indicating poor fit/sealing as more critical compared to other potential causes like type of contact surfaces alone when it comes to preventing food impaction near newly placed fillings such as those described here involving #47.
